zh.lifeSummary: 當Lucia de Clifford於大約1138年出生在英國英格蘭賀福爾克利福德時，她的父親Walter De Clifford26歲，母親Margaret de Tosny25歲。她於1152年與Hugh de Saye在英國賀福爾克利福德結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1195年在英國賀福爾享年59歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clifford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: habitational name from any of various places called Clifford in Devon, Gloucestershire, and Yorkshire, and in particular Herefordshire. The placename is derived from Old English clif ‘slope’ + ford ‘ford’.

Irish: adoption of the name in 1 above as Anglicization of several Irish names, especially Ó Clúmháin ‘descendant of Clúmhán’, which was the surname of an ecclesiastical family in Sligo and can be traced back to the 12th century. The personal name meant ‘little hairy one’, a diminutive of Irish clúmach ‘hairy’, from clúmh ‘feathers, plumage, down; hair or fur’. Clifford was also adopted for Coleman and in Fermanagh for Crifferty, Clifferty, and Cliffordy, which are Anglicized forms of Mac Raibheartaigh (compare Rafferty ).

History: A powerful Anglo-Norman family of this name in England and Ireland trace their descent from Walter de Clifford, who took the name from Clifford (Castle) in Herefordshire in the 12th century, after acquiring the Clifford barony by marriage.

variants.name: Coleman
meanings: Irish and English: from the Middle English personal name Col(e)man, Old Irish Colmán, earlier Columbán, adopted as Old Norse Kalman. It was introduced into Cumbria, Westmorland, and Yorkshire by Norwegians from Ireland and probably spread widely across England. Ó Colmáin (‘descendant of Colmán’) was the name of an Irish missionary to Europe, also known as Saint Columban(us) (c. 540–615), who founded the monastery of Bobbio in northern Italy in 614. Columbanus is formally a derivative of the Latin for ‘dove’, seen in the name of the 6th-century missionary known in English as Saint Columba (521–597), who converted the Picts to Christianity. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
Irish: from Mac Colmáin or Ó Colmáin ‘son (or descendant) of Colmán’.
Americanized form of Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Kalman   or   Kolman  .

nameMeanings.name: Lucia
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Feminine form of the old Roman given name Lucius, which is probably a derivative of Latin lux ‘light’. The girl's name is common in Italy and elsewhere, and is found as a learned, Latinate doublet of Lucy in England. St Lucia of Syracuse, who was martyred in 304 , was a very popular saint in the Middle Ages; she is often represented in medieval art as blinded and with her eyes on a platter, but the tradition that she had her eyes put out is probably based on nothing more than the association between light and eyes.

en.lifeSummary: When Lucia de Clifford was born about 1138, in Clifford, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Walter De Clifford, was 26 and her mother, Margaret de Tosny, was 25. She married Hugh de Saye in 1152, in Clifford, Herefordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She died about 1195, in Herefordshire, England, at the age of 59.

Elle a épousé Hugh de Saye en 1152, à Clifford, Herefordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée environ 1195, à Herefordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 59 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Lucia de Cliffordが約1138年にイギリスイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアクリフォードで生まれた時，父Walter De Cliffordは26歳，母Margaret de Tosnyは25歳であった。1152年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアクリフォードでHugh de Sayeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は約1195年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアで59歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lucia de Clifford(이)가 대략 1138년 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 클리퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Walter De Clifford(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret de Tosny(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1152년 영국 허퍼드실르 클리퍼드에서 Hugh de Saye(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1195년 영국 허퍼드실르에서 59세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lucia de Clifford родилась около 1138 г. в местности клиффорд, хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Walter De Clifford было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret de Tosny, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Hugh de Saye в 1152 г. в местности клиффорд, хирефордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла около 1195 г. в местности хирефордшир, Англия в возрасте 59 лет.
